meaning: give money for what you owe
example:
He paid the bill at the restaurant.
Let me pay the bill.
Who's paying the bill?

I need to make a phone call.
She made a quick phone call.
Can I make a phone call?

Take your medicine twice a day.
He took some medicine.
Don't forget to take your medicine.

I'll do the dishes after dinner.
It's your turn to do the dishes.
He's doing the dishes.

I have a shower every morning.
She's having a shower.
I'll have a quick shower.

Make your bed before school.
I made the bed this morning.
He never makes his bed.

I take my exam on Monday.
She took the exam twice.
Are you ready to take the exam?

meaning: move your body to stay fit
example:
You should do some exercise.
I do exercise every day.
Doing exercise helps you sleep.

meaning: experience images while sleeping; or a hope
example:
I had a strange dream.
She has a dream of travelling.
I had a dream about you.

We pay rent every month.
He can't pay the rent.
Rent is paid on the first.

Make a shopping list.
I made a list of tasks.
Let's make a list.

I'll take a quick nap.
She took a nap after lunch.
The baby is taking a nap.

I do the shopping on Saturdays.
Can you do the shopping?
She did the weekly shopping.

We had a long conversation.
Let's have a conversation about it.
They had a quiet conversation.

Can you keep a secret?
She kept the secret for years.
I'll keep your secret.

meaning: give a reason to avoid something
example:
He made an excuse to leave.
Stop making excuses.
She made a poor excuse.

meaning: breathe in slowly to calm down
example:
Take a deep breath and relax.
He took a deep breath.
Take a deep breath before you speak.

I'm doing an online course.
She did a cooking course.
He's doing a language course.

They're going to have a baby.
She had a baby boy.
My sister had a baby.

meaning: go to see someone or somewhere
example:
Let's pay her a visit.
We paid a visit to the museum.
He paid us a visit.

Please don't make a noise.
The kids are making a noise.
Something made a strange noise.

meaning: do something to deal with a problem
example:
We must take action now.
They took quick action.
It's time to take action.

Too much sun can do harm.
It won't do any harm.
The storm did a lot of harm.

Have a look at this.
Can I have a look?
Let me have a look.

Keep calm and carry on.
Try to keep calm.
She kept calm under pressure.

Let's make a plan for the weekend.
We made a plan.
It's good to make a plan.

The shop makes good money.
How do they make money?
She makes money online.

Volunteering does a lot of good.
A holiday will do you good.
It does good to help others.

Do you have time to talk?
I don't have time today.
We have plenty of time.

Can you give me a hand?
He gave me a hand.
Give us a hand, please.
